summary: Powerful second set with smoking China>Rider and a soaring Other One
blurb: The second set shines with an inspired China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider segue and a transcendent Space > The Other One jam. Jerry’s guitar remains thick despite rough health, while the rhythm section drives the high‑energy flow. Highlights also include Wharf Rat and a spirited Day Tripper encore.
review: December 28, 1984 at the San Francisco Civic Auditorium captured the Dead in a gritty yet exhilarating mood.  The first set delivered solid work – Touch of Grey opened with fresh vocal mic work and classics like Greatest Story Ever Told – but it’s the second set that truly lifts the night.  A smoking China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider transition erupts into a rolling jam, leading into Looks Like Rain, a tight Spoonful, then an expansive Space > The Other One stretch that drives the band forward with thunderous drums and thick, heavy grooves.  The momentum carries through Wharf Rat and culminates in a jubilant Sugar Magnolia before the encore’s bright Day Tripper.  Jerry, though described as “in sad shape,” still conjures impassioned leads and his signature thick tone; Phil anchors with solid bass and adds vocals on Touch of Grey, while Bob’s rhythm work stays rock‑steady despite a quick entrance into The Other One.  Mickey’s drumming is especially heavy, propelling the jams, and the overall energy remains high despite the band’s personal challenges.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Four of the seven recordings are clearly positive, praising standout moments like China>Rider and Space > The Other One, while only two mixed and one negative review temper the enthusiasm. The consistent acclaim for key jams yields a moderate positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Audience tape offers clear vocal/instrument separation, minimal dropouts, and captures the high‑energy crowd vibe while delivering strong overall sound quality.

key_highlights: Smoking China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider segue that ignites the second set
Transcendent Space > The Other One jam driving the show's momentum
Thick, heavy drum work from Mickey propelling the jams
High‑energy encore with a spirited Day Tripper despite Jerry’s rough condition

band_performance.Jerry: Despite being in 'sad shape' and battling personal issues, Jerry still delivered impassioned leads with his trademark thick tone throughout the show.
band_performance.Phil: Phil kept a steady bass foundation and even took on vocals for Touch of Grey, supported by a new mic.
band_performance.Bob: Bob's rhythm work remained solid; his quick entrance into The Other One was noted but overall he anchored the jams well.
band_performance.Mickey: Mickey’s drumming was thick and heavy, driving the second‑set jams—especially evident during Spoonful and the Space > The Other One transition.
